skill.name: database-manager
skill.description: Guides Supabase PostgreSQL schema changes, migrations, RLS policies, query examples, and troubleshooting for CookMode V2.
skill.summary: Manage CookMode V2 Supabase schema, migrations, policies, and database troubleshooting.
skill.author: AdamFehse

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution, Windows SAM, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance alerts are false positives from Markdown backticks, SQL examples, and ordinary database terms. The confirmed concern is permissive Supabase RLS guidance that grants all users broad read and write access, so publication is acceptable only with a clear security warning.

medium_findings.title: Permissive Row Level Security Policies
medium_findings.description: The skill documents and templates RLS policies using FOR ALL USING (true), which allows broad read and write access. This can expose or corrupt shared data outside a trusted single-team environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly states that current RLS is permissive and shows policies that allow all operations. This is legitimate for trusted kitchen use, but it is a real security risk for broader deployments.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The static findings for shell execution, Windows SAM access, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance are caused by Markdown backticks, SQL snippets, file paths, and database terminology. I did not find evidence of executable shell commands, Windows registry access, cryptographic implementation, or host/network probing in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirms these locations are Markdown documentation or SQL and JavaScript examples. They are not code that the skill executes, but marketplace readers should understand the static score was inflated by syntax-only matches.

dangerous_patterns.title: All-Access RLS Policy Template
dangerous_patterns.description: The migration template recommends CREATE POLICY statements with FOR ALL USING (true). Reusing this pattern in a multi-user application can bypass intended tenant or user boundaries.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact policy text is present in migration examples. The risk depends on deployment context, but the access control pattern is clearly permissive.

content.value_statement: CookMode V2 database work needs consistent schema changes, migrations, and RLS policy guidance. This skill gives focused Supabase PostgreSQL instructions for tables, queries, real-time sync, and troubleshooting.
actual_capabilities: Explains the CookMode V2 Supabase table model and current schema files.
Provides SQL patterns for creating tables, indexes, constraints, and migrations.
Guides Row Level Security setup and identifies when stricter policies are needed.
Shows Supabase client query patterns for select, upsert, delete, and real-time workflows.
Helps troubleshoot sync failures, constraint errors, RLS blocking, and publication settings.
limitations: It is tailored to CookMode V2 and may not fit unrelated Supabase projects.
It includes permissive RLS examples that need hardening for multi-tenant systems.
It does not run migrations or verify production database state by itself.
It depends on separate project files for full application context.

use_cases.title: Add a Shared Recipe Table
use_cases.description: Plan a new table, indexes, migration notes, and real-time hook updates for a CookMode feature.

use_cases.title: Review RLS Policy Scope
use_cases.description: Compare trusted-kitchen access with stricter user-specific policies before publishing a database change.

use_cases.title: Debug Supabase Sync Issues
use_cases.description: Trace failed real-time updates through subscriptions, RLS rules, constraints, and client connection setup.

prompt_templates.title: Explain the Current Schema
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner orientation before making database changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the database-manager skill to explain the current CookMode V2 Supabase tables and how they relate to recipe workflow state.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Migration Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate feature planning for a new table.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the database-manager skill to plan a migration for a new recipe notes table, including columns, indexes, rollback notes, and hook updates.

prompt_templates.title: Troubleshoot a Sync Failure
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate debugging of real-time state updates.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the database-manager skill to diagnose why recipe status changes are saved but do not appear in other clients.

prompt_templates.title: Harden Access Controls
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced security review before broader deployment.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the database-manager skill to replace permissive RLS guidance with user-specific policies for a multi-tenant CookMode deployment.

output_examples.input: Add notes for each recipe and keep them synced.
output_examples.output: A migration plan with a recipe notes table, a recipe slug index, RLS considerations, real-time subscription updates, and rollback guidance.

output_examples.input: Recipe status updates are not syncing between screens.
output_examples.output: A troubleshooting checklist covering real-time publication, table policies, client connection state, browser errors, and relevant Supabase queries.

output_examples.input: Prepare this database for multiple teams.
output_examples.output: A security review that flags all-access RLS policies and recommends tenant-scoped policy changes before launch.

best_practices: Back up production data and test migrations with realistic records before applying schema changes.
Replace permissive RLS policies with user-specific or tenant-specific rules outside trusted environments.
Update application hooks and documentation whenever schema or data access patterns change.
anti_patterns: Do not reuse FOR ALL USING true policies for public or multi-tenant deployments.
Do not drop tables or columns without a tested rollback and backup plan.
Do not change database constraints without checking existing data for violations.

faq.question: Does this skill execute database commands?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples. A user or tool must apply any migration separately.

faq.question: Is the included RLS policy safe for every deployment?
faq.answer: No. The all-access policy is only suitable for trusted environments and should be hardened for broader use.

faq.question: Can this skill help with Supabase real-time issues?
faq.answer: Yes. It outlines checks for subscriptions, publications, policies, connection state, and client errors.

faq.question: Can I use it outside CookMode V2?
faq.answer: Partly. General Supabase patterns may help, but table names and workflows are CookMode-specific.

faq.question: Does it cover rollback planning?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends adding manual rollback notes and testing migrations before deployment.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
